SCR:035200 Create New Safe Deposit AccountChapter 4 SDV LOCKERS                                                                                                                                      Page|  270

1. Select N: New Locker Account from the drop down menu  
2. Input the Locker ID and Cabinet ID 
3. Click the verify button and the Category Code will be fetched automatically.  
4.  Sub  Cat:  Select  the  appropriate  Locker  Sub  Category  from  the  drop  down  menu – SEMI-
URBAN/RURAL OR URBAN/METRO AND STAFF/SEMI-URBAN/RURAL OR STAFF/URBAN/METRO for 
correct Locker Rent recovery 
 5. Account Type: By Default, it will be 1. Safe Deposit  
6. Mode of fee: A/c transfer or cash. Branches are advised to choose any one of the two methods 
for proper locker rent recovery  
7. Under ‘Rent Recovery Account’ the user has to input the concerned SB account number of the 
locker account holder  
8. No. of Yrs for Which Fee Is Collected: default ‘0’ Branch user has to input number of years’ fee 
to be collected. 
 
 
Fee Waived: Always it must be “No”. If ‘Yes’ is selected, then a popup message will be displayed 
as follows 
 "This locker account rent is waived. Please ensure that the locker is used for official purpose and 
not allotted to customer’s/staff members".Chapter 4 SDV LOCKERS                                                                                                                                      Page|  271

On clicking ‘OK’, the system will display a queue and the same is to be authorized. In case Fee 
waived is given ‘Yes’, additionally, the system expects supervisory override at a capability level of 
15 and above and accordingly the pop-up will be displayed and the same is to be authorized. 
 
 
 
 
On authorization of the Queue, 10 DIGITS Locker A/ c Number will be displayed which has to be 
noted down by the Branch User in all the records and ensure collection of locker rent through the 
screen shown below. 
4.4 SDV Fee Collection 
Menu: SDV/ SC -> Financial Transaction Prompt 
After opening of account, Branch should collect the locker rent through the menu SDV (Lockers) 
>> Financial Transaction Prompt \\ SCR:035250 SDV: Prompt for fee 
Screen \\SCR 035250 ‘SDV: Prompt for Fee’ appears.  
Field ‘SDV/ SC Account No’: enter the account number.  
Field ‘Mode of Pay’: select the appropriate choice e.g., ‘1. Cash’/ ‘2. Transfer’/ ‘3. Transfer from 
Loan’/ ‘4. Transfer from BGL’ from the Drop Down Box.Chapter 4 SDV LOCKERS                                                                                                                                      Page|  272  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
 
Note: If Transfer from some Fee-bearing Account is mentioned above, that account should already 
exist.  
Field ‘Charge Collection’: select the appropriate choice e.g., ‘N: No’ from the Drop Down Box. This

Field ‘Refund Txn’: select the appropriate choice e.g., ‘N: No’ from the Drop Down Box. Select ‘Yes’ 
in case it is a Refund transaction, where the Bank pays back some amount to the customer. The 
system calculates how much is to be refunded.  
Click on ‘Transmit’.  
Depending on the choice made in the Field ‘Mode of Pay’, the relevant Screen opens. Cash and 
Transfer are displayed below: Fill in the details and click on ‘Transmit’ to complete the Transaction. 
 
 
 
Cash: Screen \\SCR: 023010 ‘G/L SDV: Cash Posting’ is opened if ‘1. Cash’ had been selected as 
the option in Field ‘Mode of Pay’ above.

Field ‘Amount’: enter the Fees being collected.  
Field ‘SDV/ SC Account No’: would be populated. 
Field ‘Statement Narrative’: mention appropriately,  
eg., ‘SDV Charges’. Click on ‘Transmit’.Chapter 4 SDV LOCKERS                                                                                                                                      Page|  273

5.1 Cash Operations 
Cash operation in CBS involves the following basic activities: 
 Opening and Closing of Cash Drawers 
 Receive cash for the day’s operations by cash officer 
 Cash deposit*  
 Cash Payments*  
 Issue of Cash to Teller 
 Return of Cash by Teller 
 Swapping of cash Denominations 
 Cash drawer Updation 
 Manual Cash Adjustments** 
 Transferring of cash to the cash vault by cash officer 
 Cash Balance book 
 Cash Teller Reports 
 
Open Cash Drawer: 
On Clicking the ‘Open Cash Drawer’ from the widget, the system will display the Currency, Opening 
Balance, Current Balance and whether currency is reconciled with the denominations on hand. The 
user has to select the currency details and click the ‘Reconcile Currency’ button. The Cash Officer 
and the Teller have to do this operation, as soon as they log into the Cash Module every day. 
 
The information regarding Branch CBS, Teller no, branch Cash drawer’s open and close date with 
time and  its opening  and  current balance will be  appeared. Before starting  cash operation, the 
cash drawer to be opened in fresh to make smooth cash reconciliation on daily basis. 
 
 
 
Receiving of Cash for The Day’s Operations by Cash officer: 
Any teller performing the duty of Cash Officer for the day will receive cash to the extent required 
for the day’s operations (previous day’s closing cash balance) in the BGL account (98903xxxxxx) 
- Branch Cash Balance account through the following menu: 
Menu: Branch Accounting (BGL) >> Credits >> Cash Posting. 
Screen \\ SCR: 020010 G/L: Cash Posting would be displayed. 
 
 
 
            5. Cash Operations and Transaction PostingChapter 5 TRANSACTION POSTING                                                                                                                   Page|  294  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
FIELD ACTION TO BE PERFORMED/DETAILS TO BE ENTERED 
Account No Account number to be entered 
Amount Transaction amount to be entered 
Narration Transaction description details to be entered 
Commission Commission to be entered wherever applicable   
Denomination screen The details of notes and coins can be entered in respective field 
Reference number Applicable for Reconcilable BGLs 
Remittance Type Applicable for DD and other remittances 
 
Click on Transmit. The system would pop up a message ‘Commission is 0”. Click OK. “Cash Drawer 
– Issue and Receive cash – Web page dialog” Screen would be displayed. The Cash Officer has to 
enter the amount of cash, denomination-wise i.e. either the actual number of notes or amount in 
each denomination in Denomination in/Amount in Field.  
 
Click on Transmit or press the enter key. The system will generate a queue, which needs to be 
authorized by an Officer necessarily by the joint holder of cash vault and upon which the system 
will display an ‘OK message’ and a ‘Journal number’. 
 
After authorization of the queue, the cash drawer of the Cash Officer would get populated with the 
amount of cash transferred and would tally with the physical cash transferred. The Cash Officer 
can then commence operations for the day by transferring cash to needy tellers. 
 
Issue of Cash by Cash Officer to Teller: 
Cash Officer has to give cash to Teller so that he will able to make payments. 
Menu: Transactions Posting >> Cash Maintenance >> Exchange Cash >> Issue Cash to Teller. 
Screen \\ Cash Issue without G/L update would be displayed. 
 
 
The amount to be transferred and the Receiving Teller’s ID to be entered. This will prompt Web 
Page Dialog box seeking input on denominations of currency. After keying the required figure in 
the  denomination  out/amount  out  column,  ensure  the  Give/Receive field  should  be ‘0’. The 
denominations given in the screen should match with the amount entered in the main screen.  
In case of any cash transaction, this is an important observation so that there will be not be any 
shortage/excess.  
Click on Transmit.  
The dialog box will close automatically. 
The system will display the message ‘Cash Drawer Updated’ on the status bar.  
Click on close to exit the screen. 
Now  the  cash which  is transferred  to  be  received  by  the  teller  to  whom  the  cash  has  been 
transferred. 
 
Receive Cash from Teller to Officer: 
Menu:  Transactions  Posting >>  Cash  Maintenance  >>  Exchange  Cash  >>  Receive  Cash  from 
Teller. 
SCR: \\ Receive composite cash with G/L Cash posting will appear. 
Click on refresh. The amount given by the Cash Officer would be displayed waiting for acceptance. 
Click on accept. Now click on the menu View Cash Drawer and click on Refresh to see the current 
balance of cash.Chapter 5 TRANSACTION POSTING                                                                                                                   Page|  295  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
 
 
Cash Swap: 
Cash swap feature is used to exchange the denominations available in cash drawer. 
Menu: Transactions Posting >> Cash Maintenance >> Cash Swap (Denomination Exchange) 
SCR: \\ Cash Drawer: Denomination Swap.  
Click on refresh.  
The  system  will  display  the  denominations  and  Total  Till  Balance.  (Till  is  Cash  Drawer  in  CBS 
balance). 
Enter  the  denominations, you  wish  to  give  out  in  the  Denomination  Out  field  and  key  in  the 
currency being received in the ‘Denomination In’ field. Also ensure that Total Till Balance and New 
Till Balance are equal. The same is true of ‘Total Amount In’ and ‘Total Amount Out’.  
Click on transmit.  
The changes will reflect immediately on the screen.Chapter 5 TRANSACTION POSTING                                                                                                                   Page|  296  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
Return of Cash by Teller to Cash Officer: 
At the end of the day, all Tellers hand over the cash to the Cash Officer.  
Menu: Transactions Posting >> Cash Maintenance >> Exchange Cash >> Issue Cash to Teller. 
 
SCR: \\ Cash Issue without G/L update appears. Enter the amount and Teller ID (In this case, the 
Cash  Officer’s  ID)  and  click  on  Transmit.  The  web  page  dialog  is  opened.  Now  key-in  the 
amount/denominations. Please check that there is no difference and click on transmit. 
 
Note: If the amount entered in the first screen is in excess of what is actually available in the Cash 
Drawer, the following message would be displayed: 
“Not enough cash in the cash drawer to carry out this transaction”. 
Now the Cash Officer has to accept this through the following menu. 
Menu: Transactions  Posting >>  Cash  Maintenance  >>  Exchange  Cash  >>  Receive  Cash  from 
Teller. 
Click on refresh. The screen now shows the denominations, pieces and total amount waiting for 
acceptance. If the amount shown on screen tallies with what was actually received, click on Accept. 
The current cash position can be viewed by clicking on View Cash Drawer and again clicking on 
Refresh. 
 
 
Close Cash Drawer by Teller and Cash Officer: 
Menu: Transactions Posting >> Cash Maintenance >> Exchange Cash >> Open and Close Cash 
Drawer. 
Click on INR to select the currency. Click on Reconcile Currency. The Currency reconciled status 
will become Yes. Now click on Close Cash Drawer. 
Until the Cash Officer accepts the receipt of cash from the Teller, the Teller will not be able to close 
his Cash Drawer. The following message is displayed in such a situation: 
 
“Could not close Cash Drawer as some cash transactions are pending with/from you.” 
 
For  Cash  Officer:  If  the  currency  is  not  reconciled  with  the  hand  balance,  then  the  following 
message appears: 
 
“All Currencies must be reconciled before cash drawer could be closed” 
 
The Cash Officer cannot close his Cash Drawer unless all the Tellers in the Branch have closed 
their respective Cash Drawers. The following is the message that appears when some Tellers have 
not closed their cash drawers: 
 
“Cash Drawer is OPEN for some teller (s)” 
 
This situation implies that some cashiers have finished their work, but did not invoke the option 
to  close  the  cash  drawer.  The  Cash  Officer  and  Vault  Custodian  have  a  special  menu  for  this 
purpose.Chapter 5 TRANSACTION POSTING                                                                                                                   Page|  297  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
Lock/Unlock Cash Drawer:  
Cash Administration >> Lock/Unlock Cash Drawer 
The only information required is the Teller ID whose cash drawer is to be closed. This option will 
run only when the balance in cash drawer of the teller is zero. To check out who are all the Tellers 
who have not closed their cash drawers, the Cash Officer can use the option. 
 
View Cash Summary: 
Reports >> Cash Related >> View Cash Summary.  
Click on fetch. Details such as Teller No., Opening  Balance, Cash Received, Cash Paid, Closing 
Balance, Currency and Status of Cash Drawer are displayed on the screen. 
At EOD the cash drawer of all tellers to be in closed status to generate error free cash balance 
book for next day. 
 
View Pending Cash Acknowledgement: 
Normally as soon as cash is received physically by the Cashier/Teller, the Cash Officer will expect 
him/her to acknowledge receipt. If the Cash Officer has to view all the cash disbursements made 
by him that has not yet been acknowledged by the Tellers, he has to invoke the following menu: 
Reports >> View Pending Cash Acknowledgment 
 
Transferring of Cash to The Cash Vault by Cash Officer. 
 
Evening Activity: 
In the Evening the Cash Officer after receiving cash from all the tellers would consolidate Cash for 
placing in the vault. He has to ensure that physical cash and system cash are tallying. Once the 
amount of physical cash to be transferred to the cash vault is arrived at, the following transaction 
has to be carried out by the Cash Officer. 
Menu: Branch Accounting (BGL) >> Debits >> Cash. 
Screen \\ SCR: 020060 G/L: Cash Disbursement would be displayed. 
 
FIELD ACTION TO BE PERFORMED/DETAILS TO BE ENTERED 
Account No Transaction amount to be entered 
Amount Transaction description to be entered 
Narration Commission to be entered wherever applicable   
Commission The details of notes and coins can be entered in respective field 
Denomination 
screen 
Once the BGL 98903 entered the Denomination screen appears to enter 
available physical cash with Cashier. 
Reference number Applicable for Reconcilable BGLs 
Remittance Type Applicable for DD and other remittances 
 
Click on Transmit or press the enter key. The system will generate a queue which needs to be 
authorized by an officer depending upon the amount of the transaction. After authorization of the 
queue, system will generate a queue which needs to be accepted by the Cash Officer. Thereafter, 
the “Cash drawer – Issue and Receive cash – Web page dialog” Screen will be opened. The Cash 
Officer has to enter the amount of withdrawal in respective denominations – either the number of 
notes or amount in each denomination in Denomination Out/Amount Out Field. 
 
Click on Transmit or press the enter key. After transmitting, the cash drawer of the Cash Officer 
would become 0 and a message “Cash Drawer Updated => OK” would be displayed. The passing 
officer has to ensure that the amount tallies with the amount of physical cash being kept in-side 
the Vault by the Cash Officer along with joint holder of cash vault for the day.Chapter 5 TRANSACTION POSTING                                                                                                                   Page|  298  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
Cash deposits and Payments: 
The cash receipts and payments is discussed in Transaction Posting chapter in detail. 
Cash Balance Book: 
After receiving cash from all the tellers, Cash Officer would consolidate the cash and ensure that 
there is no difference in the physical cash held by the teller and system cash balances. Once all 
the A&L cash, BGL cash (cash drawer) and Physical cash are tallied then cash balance book to be 
saved by cashier before doing BGL debit 98903.  
 
In New Banking  Channel (NBC) under navigation Home → Reports → Reports → Cash Balance 
Book Click the ‘Today’ button, the cash balance book will be available at CDC. Branches should 
ascertain that, closing physical cash tallies with the BGL cash balance, which in turn tallies with 
A&L cash balance then cash balance book Report should be generated before authorisation of BGL 
98903 debit queue by using the above said navigation. 
                                        
 
Report  generation  is  enabled  for  both  tellers/Officer.  On  clicking  transmit  button,  a  success

Please note that this kind of event is very exceptional. Transaction to handle this event, can be

custodian.

etc. that the physical cash balance is correct and that system cash balance is short. 
Now the Vault custodian should proceed as follows: 
 
Transaction Posting >> Cash Maintenance >> Cash drawer operations. 
 
Give teller no. of the concerned  cashier, click on the row that appears till it becomes blue and

click once more on the box where the teller number was keyed and execute again.

increased by the appropriate amount.

View Journal Screen: 
In any cash transaction, the status of transaction can be verified at “View Journal screen” which 
is available at top right corner (widgets list) of NBC screen. The status of cash transaction can be 
verified at any time. It may be “Committed/rejected/failed/supervisor override/Referred”  
 
 If transaction status is ‘Committed’ then it will be treated as Successful transaction. 
 If transaction status is ‘Supervisor override’ and ‘Committed’, then it will be treated as 
Success transaction where the transaction is completed through supervisor override.  
 If  transaction status is ‘Supervisor override’ and ‘Pending’, then it will be treated as 
pending  at  teller  level for  cash  acceptance/out  for  delivery  of  cash.  After  doing  cash 
acceptance at teller level, the transaction will become committed and supervisor override. 
 If  transaction status is ‘Failed’, initiate the transaction after verifying transaction enquiry 
screen if necessary. 
 If  transaction status is ‘Referred’,  then  complete  the  transaction  through  either  by  cash 
correction if cash drawer not updated  or reject the transaction and  initiate the  transaction 
again  based  on  transaction  enquiry  screen.  In  some  user  may  face  error “7000:0:  CashChapter 5 TRANSACTION POSTING                                                                                                                   Page|  301

Drawer not updated because entry already exists” it indicates cash drawer already updated 
and the same can be ensured in cash reports.

5.7 Cash withdrawal restriction 
As system control on cash withdrawal in SBCA of government deposit product, the daily Cash 
withdrawal  limit  for  Rs.200000/- set  for  government  account  product  SB  2311 -1561  AND  CA 
2311-1561. Beyond the limit, ZO approval has to be obtained for withdrawal of cash. The system 
will through error as “Error 7000: DAILY LIMIT EXCEED ZO APPR ON 7014 SCR Rs: 899 “. 
Process Flow:  
1. When a  user  performs  cash  withdrawal  transaction  (screen  no:  51072)  system  will  check, 
validate the amount for per day limit of Rs.200000/-  
2. If aggregate of transaction  amount  and  earlier  total cash withdrawal amount for  the day is 
below 200000 i.e. daily limit, then system will allow transaction as per existing process  
3. If the above exceeds the daily limit of Rs.200000, then system will throw a supervisory override 
to the effect “Exceeds daily withdrawal Limit”. (Level 15) 
4. Then Branch has to take approval for the transaction through Screen no. 7014 following are 
the field display at branch level 
 
At Branch: 
 
 
After  transmit  following  screen will opt account number and  then 7016 screen will open where 
Cheque number, referral reason and requested amount to be entered. After transmit the queue 
will be approved by checker at branch level. 
 
 
At Zonal Office: 
Once request placed by branch, the same will be available at respective ZO 42 user for approval 
in screen 7015. Now Zo 42 user will enter account number in screen 7014 as shown below. 
 
After transmit the details entered by branch will be available for enquiry.Chapter 5 TRANSACTION POSTING                                                                                                                   Page|  333  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
 
ZO user can either approve or reject by selecting dropdown. 
 
 
Once approved the journal will be generated, then branch can proceed with 51072 screen for cash 
withdrawal as per existing procedure. 
 
NOTE:  
1. If the entry done by is in pending status, then system allows any amendment and deletion at 
branch level. 
2. If the entry done by branch is in other than pending status i.e. approved or rejected - and the 
said input done by branch is incorrect then branch has to take up with zonal office for deletion 
of the entry ( for e.g. branch has given cheque no. correctly but amount entered is wrong and 
entry is passed by ZO and entry status is approved or rejected  then system will not allow to 
enter  fresh entry with same cheque number hence for update correct entry request to be 
given to zo for deletion ( which is procedurally handle)

5.10 Core to Core Transaction 
These transactions are carried out through BGL accounts and follow the ‘Maker-Checker’ 
concept, irrespective of the amount of transactions. 
The erstwhile Branch General Ledger (BGL) heads are used under the revised Core to Core Inter 
Branch reconciliation (instead of IBGA reconciliation) as under: 
BGL No TYPE BGL Name Re coined Erstwhile BGL Name 
86903 Credit Preceding C2C IL Cr to BRANCH SERV CR 2 BRANCH 
86911 Credit Preceding C2C OL Cr to BRANCH SERV BR COLLECTION 
86904 Debit Preceding C2C IL Dr to BRANCH SERV DR 2 BRANCH 
All the above accounts are self-reconcilable with one to one matching, having validation of the 
following parameters:  
 Reference Number  
 Originating date  
 Amount  
 Originating Branch code  
 Responding Branch code. 
 
 BGL 86903 for IL (Internal liabilities): BGL 86903 should be used to originate inter-branch 
credit entries of IL nature only on other branches like BP realisation, all clearing related credits 
and sundries, etc. 
 BGL 86911 for OL (Outside Liabilities): BGL 86911 should be used to originate inter-branch 
credit entries of OL nature only on other branches like OBC/FOBC realisation, etc. 
 BGL 86904 for Originating Debits: BGL 86904 should be used to originate inter-branch Debit 
entries on other branches, with restricted use of this BGL, to Service Branches / Main branches

Cash Remittances Under Core to Core (C2c)  
When cash is remitted from a branch to currency chest or feeder branch, or cash is withdrawn 
from currency chest by a branch, the following steps will be followed:  
 The paying chest/branch debits the ‘Funds in Transit’ account and sends cash.  
 At the receiving chest/branch, cash should be first received to the credit of the “Intermediary 
Parking” a/c on the same day.  
 The parking a/c should, then, be debited to create an originating credit entry in the BGL 86903 
on the remitting chest/branch.  
 This entry will be responded by the concerned Chest/Branch to reverse the debit entry in their 
Funds in Transit (FIT) on the same day.

6.2 NEFT and RTGS Payments 
RTGS and NEFT are two important Pan-India payment systems introduced by RBI, keeping in mind 
the requirements of various customers in the wholesale and  retail payment systems segment. 
Both these systems have distinct objectives and unique features in terms of the time criticality of 
payments,  threshold  value  of  transactions,  mode  of  settlement  etc.  All  our  CBS  branches  are 
enabled for sending and receiving RTGS transactions. 
(I) NEFT PAYMENTS 
NEFT service is available round the clock 24×7 and 365 days on all days of the year. It is conducted 
in batches every 30 minutes throughout the day. 
If the NEFT N06 message is returned, it will be credited to ‘NEFT Inter-bank Cash Suspense’ BGL 
(99635).

6.3 Beneficiary Account Name Lookup facility 
Beneficiary  Account Name Look-up  (BANL) facility  for Real Time Gross Settlement (RTGS) and 
National  Electronic  Funds  Transfer  (NEFT)  Systems  would  enable  a  remitter  to  verify  the 
beneficiary account name based on Account number and IFSC code before initiating a transaction 
using RTGS or NEFT like UPI and IMPS to avoid mistakes and prevent frauds. 
Fetch button is introduced for Fetching  Beneficiary Account Name in RTGS & NEFT transactions 
screens, where Beneficiary account name provided by the beneficiary bank will be displayed on 
clicking FETCH button. This is mandatory for all RTGS / NEFT transactions. 
This facility is available in NBC screens - 20078, 51025, 51026, 20074, 20066, 20054, 20035 (only 
for R41). 
 Before initiating RTGS / NEFT transactions, Beneficiary Name to be verified 
 After entering Beneficiary account number and IFSC; on clicking Fetch button, Beneficiary 
Account Name will be fetched from Beneficiary Bank through NPCI. 
 Beneficiary Name will be displayed  
 Branch Official has to verify the name with the name provided by the remitter.  
 If the name varies, it is to be informed to the customer for getting his / her consent before 
proceeding further.  It is discretion of the customer.  
 In case the beneficiary name cannot be displayed for any reason, the remitter can proceed 
further for fund transfer.  
 Without Fetching the Beneficiary Name, system will not allow to “Verify Amount” to 
proceed for TRANSMIT.

6.5 LEI Capturing 
RBI has advised banks to mandatorily capture sender and beneficiary Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) 
information  for  all  applicable  RTGS  and  NEFT  transaction  where  the  value  is  Rs.50 crores  and 
above i.e. 20-digit LEI number is mandatory for all applicable transactions.
